name: Qatar

colors.symbolism: the blood shed in the wars of Qatar; the deep purple-red was darkened from the original red over time, traditionally said to be the result of natural dyes (from sea-snails) reacting with the sun
peace; introduced by the General Treaty of Peace with the British in 1820

description: A white vertical band at the hoist, separated from a maroon field that fills the rest of the flag by a serrated (zig-zag) vertical line forming nine white triangular points pointing toward the fly. The flag is unusually elongated, with proportions 11:28.
history: Qatar's red-and-white flag dates to the mid-19th century, when the British required Gulf states under the General Treaty of Peace (1820) to fly a red flag with a white serration. After the 1916 Qatari–British treaty, the number of points was set at nine, and the red darkened to the distinctive maroon. The current proportions (11:28) and exact design were codified after independence in 1971; Qatar's flag has the most extreme aspect ratio of any national flag in the world.

features.symbolism: a maroon panel at the fly separated from a white panel at the hoist by a serrated (zig-zag) vertical line of nine points; maroon for blood shed in Qatar's many wars (especially the second half of the 19th century), white for peace, and the nine points for Qatar's accession as the ninth member of the 'reconciled emirates' of the Persian Gulf at the conclusion of the Qatari–British treaty of 1916
Qatar's status as the ninth member of the 'reconciled emirates' of the Persian Gulf following the Qatari–British treaty of 1916
